brand.name: Simplifii
brand.tagline: Built for complex briefs. Not complex brains.

rules.alignment: Left-align dense text. Avoid center-alignment for anything over 2 lines.
rules.modes: When Read Easy mode is active, switch to OpenDyslexic and leading-loose (1.8).

spacing_and_layout.base_grid: bento-style grids for features, 1-column max-w-4xl for reading mode to prevent tracking errors
spacing_and_layout.tap_targets: CRITICAL: min-h-[44px] and min-w-[44px] on ALL buttons, toggles, checkboxes, and links.
spacing_and_layout.padding: Generous spacing. Use p-6, p-8, p-12. Avoid cramped containers. Breathing room is essential.
spacing_and_layout.border_radius: rounded-2xl for outer cards, rounded-xl for inner elements (Duolingo/Notion hybrid).
spacing_and_layout.card_style: Cardless or flat 1px borders (border-border). Avoid heavy drop shadows to minimize sensory load.
spacing_and_layout.focus_management: focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-[#007C8C] focus-visible:ring-offset-2 on all focusable elements. No outline:none without a replacement.

components.buttons: Large, solid pill or slightly rounded rectangular shapes. High contrast text.

cognitive_mode_switcher.description: 4-way segmented control at the top of the Results view.

modes.name: Focus
modes.action: Shows one task at a time, 'Next Task' button

modes.name: Read Easy
modes.action: Changes font to OpenDyslexic, background to cream, line-height 1.8

modes.name: Calm
modes.action: Hides deadlines, adds supportive banner 'You've got this. One step at a time.'

modes.name: Step by Step
modes.action: Hides all context, shows only 'What do I do right now?'

overwhelm_detection_modal.trigger: User idle on results page for 3 minutes
overwhelm_detection_modal.style: Gentle, non-intrusive, soft backdrop. Text: 'Feeling overwhelmed? That's completely normal. Let's just do one thing right now.' Button: 'Show me the next step only'.

components.progress_tracking: Visual progress bars and checkboxes. Trigger subtle confetti/celebration (if motion allowed) on task complete.
components.navigation: Sticky top nav. Links: Brief Simplifier, Rubric Simplifier, Essay Scorer, Humaniser, Assessment Scaffolder, Course Planner. Right side: Credits balance and User profile.

motion_and_interactions.default_transitions: transition-all duration-200 ease-in-out for hover states.
motion_and_interactions.loading_states: Use skeleton loaders instead of spinning wheels where possible to reduce anxiety.
motion_and_interactions.reduced_motion: If user toggles 'Reduce Motion', all transition durations map to 0ms. Disable celebratory confetti.

media.rules: Use images to create a sense of calm and structure. Avoid chaotic or highly detailed stock photos.

images.description: Abstract calming geometric shapes in soft teal and cream colors, minimalist 3d shapes
Minimalist workspace illustration, neat desk with a single piece of paper, soft warm sunlight, pastel teal accents

testing.rules: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute in kebab-case.

instructions_to_main_agent: Prioritize accessibility over flashy UI. The target audience is neurodivergent users who need a calm, predictable interface.
Implement the 4 Cognitive Modes using React state or Context, wrapping the main Results component.
Use standard Tailwind classes but leverage arbitrary values for exact brand colors (e.g., bg-[#007C8C]).
Ensure the 'Accessibility Toolbar' state is saved in localStorage so preferences persist across sessions.
For the 'Overwhelm Detection', use a simple timeout effect that resets on user interaction.
Credit balance must always be visible in the navigation bar.
Use Shadcn UI for complex components (Dialogs, Selects, Dropdowns) but customize them heavily to remove the default Vercel-look. Remove borders where possible to fit the 'Notion/Linear' minimal vibe.
